heading: Appeals to the Secretary
label: 1465 Appeals to the Secretary
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (b)(3)(A)(ii)(I), was in the original “this Act” which was translated as reading “this title”, meaning title III of which is classified generally to this chapter, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 89–454

Amendments2005— amended section catchline and text generally, substituting provisions relating to notice, closure of record, and deadline for decision for provisions relating to notice, deadline for decision, and application of section.Pub. L. 109–58
amendments: Amendments2005— amended section catchline and text generally, substituting provisions relating to notice, closure of record, and deadline for decision for provisions relating to notice, deadline for decision, and application of section.Pub. L. 109–58

children.heading: Notice
children.label: a Notice
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of the filing of an appeal to the Secretary of a consistency determination under , the Secretary shall publish an initial notice in the Federal Register.section 1456 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than the end of the 160-day period beginning on the date of publication of an initial notice under subsection (a), except as provided in paragraph (3), the Secretary shall immediately close the decision record and receive no more filings on the appeal.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 2 Notice
children.text: After closing the administrative record, the Secretary shall immediately publish a notice in the Federal Register that the administrative record has been closed.

children.label: i
children.text: for a specific period mutually agreed to in writing by the appellant and the State agency; or

children.label: I
children.text: any supplemental information specifically requested by the Secretary to complete a consistency review under this chapter; or

children.label: II
children.text: any clarifying information submitted by a party to the proceeding related to information in the consolidated record compiled by the lead Federal permitting agency.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: The Secretary may only stay the 160-day period described in paragraph (1) for a period not to exceed 60 days.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of publication of a Federal Register notice stating when the decision record for an appeal has been closed, the Secretary shall issue a decision or publish a notice in the Federal Register explaining why a decision cannot be issued at that time.

children.heading: Subsequent decision
children.label: 2 Subsequent decision
children.text: Not later than 15 days after the date of publication of a Federal Register notice explaining why a decision cannot be issued within the 60-day period, the Secretary shall issue a decision.
